idea: TILE_K=128+drc+native-f16+apprx-div+ptx-barrier — double tile K to reduce loop iterations
justification: 4 consecutive discards. TILE_K=128 halves K-loop iterations (12→6), reducing TMA/barrier/foreach overhead. For small 768³ shape, per-iteration overhead dominates.

idea: WARP_M=128 — reduce CTA count from 96 to 48, each CTA does 2x M work
justification: 5 consecutive discards. Current best uses WARP_M=64 giving 12 CTAs in M-dim. WARP_M=128 gives 6 CTAs in M-dim (48 total). Fewer CTAs with more work each may reduce scheduling overhead and improve L2 reuse within each CTA.

idea: WARP_N=48 — 192 CTAs (12x16), maximum parallelism for small shape
justification: 6 consecutive discards. WARP_N=48 gives cdiv(768,64)=12 × cdiv(768,48)=16 = 192 CTAs. Small shape may benefit from maximum CTA-level parallelism to hide latency.

idea: WARP_N=192 (48 CTAs, very large N tile)
justification: 7 consecutive discards. WARP_N=192 gives cdiv(768,64)=12 x cdiv(768,192)=4 = 48 CTAs. Fewer CTAs with much larger tiles may reduce scheduling overhead.

idea: RHS swiz<64> instead of swiz<128> — smaller swizzle for better bank distribution on small shape
justification: 7 consecutive discards. Current best uses RHS swiz<128>. For small 768³ shape with WARP_N=96, swiz<64> may reduce SMEM bank conflicts on the RHS tile load.

idea: RHS swiz<64> instead of swiz<128>
justification: 8 consecutive discards. RHS swiz<64> may reduce bank conflicts for small shape.

idea: --maxrregcount=128 to reduce register pressure and improve occupancy
justification: 8 consecutive discards. The persistent bottleneck is occupancy_limited. Reducing max registers per thread may increase active warps per SM, improving occupancy and hiding latency.

idea: Direct global store instead of TMA output — eliminate TMA overhead for small output tile
justification: 8 consecutive discards. Current best uses tma.copy for output store. For small 768x768 output, direct global store (stmatrix or st.global) may eliminate TMA setup overhead.

idea: dma.copy output instead of tma.copy
justification: 9 consecutive discards. TMA output store overhead for small 768x768 output.

idea: WARP_K=64 instead of WARP_K=32 — halves inner loop iterations from 2 to 1
justification: 9 consecutive discards. Current WARP_K=32 creates 2 inner loop iterations (cdiv(64,32)=2). WARP_K=64 eliminates the inner loop entirely, reducing foreach overhead and barrier sync points.

idea: WARP_N=256 (24 CTAs, maximum N tile)
justification: 10 consecutive discards. Radical tile shape change — maximum N tile.

idea: WARP_N=80 EDSL tile variant from current best to probe compute-bound tile width sensitivity
justification: Current best at WARP_N=96 indicates compute-bound behavior. Radical tile-width sweep around 96 may improve WGMMA packing and CTA scheduling without the crash-prone TILE_K/LHS-swizzle changes.

idea: WARP_N=112 legal sparse-MMA tile-width probe from current best
justification: Compute-bound small square remains below the iter82 local optimum; this distinct legal tile/epilogue variant tests whether a different sparse-MMA shape or store-path tradeoff can improve throughput.
